name: Sakha (Yakutia)

colors.symbolism: the clear skies, the cold winters, and the spirit of the Sakha (Yakut) people
the long northern winter, snow, and purity
courage, beauty and the friendship of the peoples of Sakha
the taiga, summer pastures, and the renewal of nature

description: A horizontal banner with a broad blue field above three narrow stripes -- white, red and green -- in approximate proportions 12:1:1:2. A large white disc is centred on the blue field, representing the sun.
history: The flag of the Sakha Republic (Yakutia) was adopted on 14 October 1992 by the Supreme Soviet of the Republic, designed by a team led by Mikhail Starostin. The white sun on the blue field is taken from the republic's coat of arms.

features.symbolism: the broad blue field stands for the clear skies and the colour of the Sakha people; the narrow stripes evoke the snowy purity, hardiness and friendship of peoples (white), faith and resilience (red), and the taiga forests of Yakutia (green).
a white disc on the blue stripe representing the sun and warmth in the cold north; an emblem of life, light, and the prosperity of the Sakha people.
